paper_title: Non Linear Response of a Harmonic Diatomic Molecule: an Exact Algebraic Approach

methods.description: A Lie algebraic approach to solve the problem of a nonlinearly driven harmonic diatomic molecule by reducing it to a set of coupled nonlinear ordinary differential equations.

steps.description: Write the system's Hamiltonian as H = H0 + V, where H0 is the unperturbed harmonic oscillator Hamiltonian and V is the interaction.
steps.searchable_summary: define system Hamiltonian with unperturbed and interaction components

steps.description: Transform to the interaction picture where the time evolution operator satisfies the equation with the interaction picture Hamiltonian.
Use the Wei-Norman Theorem to write the exact time evolution operator as a product of exponentials.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Wei-Norman Theorem for time evolution operator

steps.description: Substitute into the set of first order, coupled, nonlinear differential equations for the complex functions αn(t).
steps.searchable_summary: derive coupled nonlinear differential equations for αn(t)

steps.description: Solve the differential equations numerically with initial conditions αi(t0) = 0, i = 1, ..., 6.
steps.searchable_summary: numerically solve differential equations with initial conditions

steps.description: Obtain the temporal evolution of any observable from the creation, annihilation operators in the Heisenberg picture.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate temporal evolution of observables using Heisenberg picture

steps.description: Calculate the average value of the displacement coordinate taken between number states.
steps.searchable_summary: compute average displacement coordinate between number states
